ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT COSCO FIRE PROTECTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANT COSCO FIRE PROTECTION, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED ON TWO GROUNDS. FIRST, DEFENDANT'S MOTION IS UNTIMELY. DEFENDANT'S PROOF OF SERVICE DEMONSTRATES THAT IT FAILED TO PROVIDE PROPER NOTICE AS REQUIRED BY C.C.P. SECTIONS 437C AND 1010.6. PLAINTIFFS' OPPOSITION ARGUED THAT THE MOTION WAS UNTIMELY AND THUS PLAINTIFFS DID NOT WAIVE SUCH ARGUMENT. SECOND, ALTHOUGH NOT MENTIONED ANYWHERE IN DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS, THIS IS THE SECOND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DEFENDANT HAS BROUGHT IN THIS MATTER. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT THE PROPER CIRCUMSTANCES EXIST TO BRING A SECOND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND/OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. DEFENDANT FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT NEW OR DIFFERENT FACTS, CIRCUMSTANCES OR LAW WARRANT THIS SECOND MOTION PURSUANT TO EITHER C.C.P. SECTION 1008(B) OR C.C.P. SECTION 437C(F)(2). PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. OFF THE RECORD THE COURT ON ITS OWN MOTION ORDERED THE HEARING CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 4, 2012 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503 TO DETERMINE THE AMOUNT OF SANCTIONS. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT F.P. LATHROP CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT F.P. LATHROP CONSTRUCTION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS OFF CALENDAR. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT MR. PETERSON WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND MR. CANTLEY'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. PLAINTIFFS DISMISSED THEIR STRICT LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION AND CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, WHICH WERE THE ONLY ISSUES DEFENDANT SOUGHT TO HAVE SUMMARILY ADJUDICATED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.
